title: Casey Means focuses on chronic illness and research funding in her opening statement

Spanberger says Trump didn't tell the truth in State of the Union speech12:56Watch President Trump's 2026 State of the Union address in 3 minutes03:01Trump reflects on 250 years of America in closing of State of the Union remarks04:08Trump says Iran 'working to build missiles that will soon reach the United States of America'03:16Trump praises Witkoff, Kushner and Rubio for Iran, Gaza negotiations03:55Trump urges voter ID laws nationally and calls out Mamdani03:12Watch: Lawmakers stand in support of immigration enforcement while others shout03:09Trump honors Charlie Kirk, condemns political violence 'of any kind'01:48Vance announces suspension of Medicaid payments to Minnesota01:18Chicago mayor: Trump used State of the Union speech 'to divide people'07:37GOP congressman praises 'vision from President Trump' in State of the Union speech22:04Means faces Senate questioning over qualifications and vaccines for surgeon general role03:35Mullin calls out Sanders on healthcare crisis: ‘You should have fixed this a long time ago’00:43Now PlayingCasey Means focuses on chronic illness and research funding in her opening statement06:58
summary: Dr. Casey Means, President Donald Trump’s pick for surgeon general, opened her Senate nomination hearing outlining her concerns on chronic illness in the U.S. and highlighting her experience. Means was originally set to testify in October, but the hearing was postponed after she went into labor with her first child.
